subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Go Math Texas: Beginning-of-Year Test

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Represent fractions with models / equal parts

standards.subject: math
standards.description: Tell time to the nearest minute — prerequisite

prompt: Evan has 54 pencils. He places the same number of pencils in each of 6 pencil cases. Which related fact shows the number of pencils in each pencil case?

options.rationale: 18 pencils per case would not split 54 into 6 equal groups.
This is subtraction, not the related multiplication/division fact.
Subtraction does not show equal groups of 6.

explanation: 54 ÷ 6 = 9, and the related multiplication fact is 9 × 6 = 54, so each case holds 9 pencils.

prompt: Mari is drawing a sketch for a flower garden. Each unit square is 1 square foot. Which multiplication equation can be used to find the area of the flower garden?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 172 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="8" width="26" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="60" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="8" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="112" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="138" y="86" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>

options.rationale: Area is found by multiplying, not adding, the side lengths.
The grid is 4 by 6, not 4 by 5.
The grid is not 4 by 4.

explanation: The garden is 4 rows by 6 columns of unit squares, so area = 4 × 6 = 24 square feet.

prompt: Carmen is putting tape around the edge of a poster. The poster has side lengths of 3 feet and 4 feet. How many feet of tape will Carmen need?

options.rationale: 7 is just 3 + 4 (one length and one width); a rectangle has two of each.
That doubles only one side.
12 is the area (3 × 4), not the distance around.

explanation: Perimeter of a rectangle = 2 × (length + width) = 2 × (3 + 4) = 14 feet.

prompt: Mr. Bonta ate the part of a pie that is shaded. What fraction of the pie did he eat?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 120 120" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M60,60 L60.0,10.0 A50,50 0 0 1 103.3,35.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L103.3,35.0 A50,50 0 0 1 103.3,85.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L103.3,85.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,110.0 Z" fill="#cfcfcf" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L60.0,110.0 A50,50 0 0 1 16.7,85.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L16.7,85.0 A50,50 0 0 1 16.7,35.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M60,60 L16.7,35.0 A50,50 0 0 1 60.0,10.0 Z" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figures.alt: pie in 6 equal parts with 3 shaded

options.rationale: More than one part is shaded.
Fewer than five parts are shaded.
Count the shaded parts again.

explanation: The pie has 6 equal parts and 3 are shaded, so 3/6 of the pie was eaten. [Figure shading is faint in the original scan; 3/6 (half the pie) is the most defensible reading.]

prompt: Devin earns $8 per hour at a bakery. How much does he earn in 9 hours?

options.rationale: That is 8 × 8; he works 9 hours.
That adds 8 + 9 instead of multiplying.
That is 9 × 9; he earns $8 per hour.

prompt: The number of people attending a baseball game is 1,402. Which base-ten model shows the number 1,402?

options.rationale: 1,402 has 4 hundreds, not 5.
1,402 has 0 tens; this shows a ten.
Wrong number of hundreds and tens.

explanation: 1,402 = 1 thousand + 4 hundreds + 0 tens + 2 ones, so the model has 1 large cube, 4 flats, no rods, and 2 small cubes. [The original shows block pictures; the correct one has no tens rods.]

prompt: Which set of numbers contains all odd numbers?

options.rationale: 788 is even (ends in 8).
456 is even (ends in 6).
542 is even (ends in 2).

explanation: A number is odd if its ones digit is 1, 3, 5, 7, or 9. Only 503, 217, 851 are all odd.

prompt: Which fraction names the point closest to 0 on the number line?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 380 80" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="18" y1="40" x2="362" y2="40" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#a)" marker-start="url(#b)"/><defs><marker id="a" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6" fill="black"/></marker><marker id="b" markerWidth="10" markerHeight="10" refX="0" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M6,0 L0,3 L6,6" fill="black"/></marker></defs><line x1="30.0" y1="33" x2="30.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="30.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">0</text><line x1="110.0" y1="33" x2="110.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="110.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle"></text><line x1="190.0" y1="33" x2="190.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="190.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle"></text><line x1="270.0" y1="33" x2="270.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle"></text><line x1="350.0" y1="33" x2="350.0" y2="47" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><text x="350.0" y="64" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text></svg>
figures.alt: number line 0 to 1 divided into 4 equal parts

options.rationale: 2/4 is halfway, not closest to 0.
4/4 is at 1, the farthest from 0.
3/4 is close to 1, not 0.

explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 4 equal parts. The mark closest to 0 is one part from 0, which is 1/4.

prompt: There are 2 barns on a horse ranch. Each barn has 4 rows of horse stalls. There are 20 stalls in each row. Which equation shows the Commutative Property of multiplication?

options.rationale: That shows grouping (Associative Property).
Both sides are identical; no property is shown.
That groups factors (Associative Property).

explanation: The Commutative Property changes the order of factors: 2 × 4 × 20 = 2 × 20 × 4.

prompt: Carter starts practicing piano at 3:45 P.M. He finishes 30 minutes later. At what time does he finish?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 140 140" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="70" cy="70" r="58" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="3"/><text x="92.0" y="36.9" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">1</text><text x="108.1" y="53.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">2</text><text x="114.0" y="75.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">3</text><text x="108.1" y="97.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">4</text><text x="92.0" y="113.1" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">5</text><text x="70.0" y="119.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">6</text><text x="48.0" y="113.1" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">7</text><text x="31.9" y="97.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">8</text><text x="26.0" y="75.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">9</text><text x="31.9" y="53.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">10</text><text x="48.0" y="36.9" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">11</text><text x="70.0" y="31.0" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle">12</text><line x1="70" y1="70" x2="30.0" y2="70.0" stroke="black" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="70" y1="70" x2="95.9" y2="80.7" stroke="black" stroke-width="3"/><circle cx="70" cy="70" r="3" fill="black"/></svg>

options.rationale: That goes back 30 minutes instead of forward.
That is not 30 minutes after 3:45.
That adds a whole hour, not 30 minutes.

explanation: 30 minutes after 3:45 is 4:15 (15 minutes to 4:00, then 15 more).

prompt: Jack finished 3/8 of his math problems. Which shows 3/8 written as a sum of unit fractions?

options.rationale: 2/8 is not a unit fraction.
That equals 9/8, and 3/8 is not a unit fraction.
These have different denominators and do not total 3/8.

explanation: A unit fraction has 1 on top. 3/8 = 1/8 + 1/8 + 1/8.

prompt: Deena gets a $20 allowance every month. She saves $7 of her allowance each month. How much will she save in 5 months?

options.rationale: That adds 20 + 7 once.
That is not 7 saved each of 5 months.
That is 20 × 5, her whole allowance, not her savings.

explanation: She saves the same amount each month: 7 × 5 = 35 dollars.

prompt: Katherine wants to buy 2 books. Each book costs $14. She has $20 saved and will borrow the rest from her sister. She will pay her sister back $4 each week. How many weeks will it take to pay back her sister?
explanation: Two books cost 2 × 14 = $28. She borrows 28 − 20 = $8. At $4 per week, 8 ÷ 4 = 2 weeks.

prompt: Drew has 53 trading cards. He gives 18 to his brother. How many trading cards does he have left?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 42 42" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figures.alt: strip diagram: 53 total, 18 given away, ? left

prompt: Brett stacked 15 boxes of markers. He places his stacks in 3 rows with the same number of boxes in each row. How many boxes are in each row?

options.rationale: That is the number of rows, not boxes per row.
6 × 3 = 18, too many.

explanation: Share equally into 3 rows: 15 ÷ 3 = 5 boxes per row.

prompt: Brooke folds her socks in sets of 2. She folds 18 socks. How many sets of socks does she fold?

options.rationale: 10 sets would be 20 socks.
8 sets would be 16 socks.
That subtracts 18 − 2 instead of dividing.

prompt: Ricardo has two $10 bills, four $1 bills, two quarters, and one dime. How much money does Ricardo have?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 150" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="10" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="43" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$10</text><rect x="84" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="117" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$10</text><rect x="158" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="191" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="232" y="10" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="265" y="32" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="10" y="54" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="43" y="76" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><rect x="84" y="54" width="66" height="34" rx="3" fill="#d6ecd6" stroke="#2a6b2a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="117" y="76" font-size="14" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1a4a1a">$1</text><circle cx="44" cy="128" r="22" fill="#d9c8a0" stroke="#7a6a3a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="44" y="132" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">25¢</text><circle cx="102" cy="128" r="22" fill="#d9c8a0" stroke="#7a6a3a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="102" y="132" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">25¢</text><circle cx="153" cy="128" r="15" fill="#d9c8a0" stroke="#7a6a3a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="153" y="132" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">10¢</text></svg>
figures.alt: two $10 bills, four $1 bills, two quarters, one dime

options.rationale: That misses some of the bills and coins.
Recount the coins: two quarters and a dime are 60¢.
Two quarters and a dime make 60¢, not 55¢.

prompt: Which measurement should be used for the volume of a container?

options.rationale: Pounds measure weight, not volume.
Inches measure length, not volume.
Grams measure mass/weight, not liquid volume.

explanation: Liquid volume (capacity) of a container is measured in liters.

prompt: The diagram shows the outline of a vegetable garden. Each unit square is 1 square foot. What is the area of the vegetable garden in square feet?

figures.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 146 146" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect x="8" y="8" width="26" height="26" fill="white" stroke="black" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="34" y="8" width="26"
